title: A Quiet Place: Day One Box Office Success
text: The horror prequel achieved a major milestone with its debut. According to reporting from the trade publication Variety, the film raked in $53 million domestically, setting the franchise record for an opening weekend. Internationally, it earned $45.5 million, bringing the worldwide total to $98.5 million. This performance surpassed previous installments in the series. The original A Quiet Place opened with $50 million, while A Quiet Place Part II debuted with $48 million during the pandemic era. Despite its strong performance, the prequel couldn't dethrone Pixar's Inside Out 2, which earned $57.4 million the same weekend and became the first movie of 2024 to surpass the $1 billion mark globally. Kevin Costner's western Horizon: An American Saga - Chapter 1 came in third with approximately $11 million. The film maintains solid reviews, holding an 84% critics score on Rotten Tomatoes, with a 73% audience score, indicating audience enthusiasm for continuing the alien-invasion narrative in this universe.
